ai_assist.reasoning_note: Keep chips to structural shell categories. Use ergonomic for non-mirrored/asymmetrical sculpted shells; use hybrid only when a credible source explicitly says hybrid or describes a symmetrical/ambi top-front mixed with ergonomic/asymmetric side/back shaping; use symmetrical for mirrored/ambi-style shells, including right-handed symmetrical mice. Keep asymmetrical/asymmetric/ergo, hand-compatibility wording, ambi wording, and generic comfortable/ergonomic marketing out of public chips unless normalized to one canonical value.

Closed placement vocabulary only. Normalize middle to center and source phrases such as forward/front/centered/middle/rearward/rear-set/back/highest point to a canonical placement value. Do not store source prose, grip recommendations, or height-only terms as chips.

Keep the public chips direction-only; normalize source degree words such as slight/moderate/aggressive away unless a future schema intentionally stores flare intensity. Do not create chips from button-splay, flared-front, taper, narrows/widens, or degree-qualified source wording.

data_lists.normalize: casefold + trim + map degree-qualified direction phrases to canonical direction

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Keep closed. Protocol names and source phrases such as 2.4GHz, Bluetooth, BLE, RF, USB receiver, Logi Bolt, Unifying, LIGHTSPEED, HyperSpeed, SLIPSTREAM, dual-mode wireless, tri-mode, wireless/wired, wired + wireless, USB-C charging/data, USB-C charging cable, receiver extension cable, and hybrid switches normalize through connectivity or guidance, not this chip list. `hybrid` means wired-data + wireless connection, not hybrid switch/sensor technology.

Use buyer-facing host connection modes only; brand-specific wireless names and source wording map to canonical chips, not new enum values. Keep wired, wired mode, USB, USB 2.0/3.0, USB Type-C/USB-C, charging/data cable, 2.4G/2.4 GHz, wireless receiver, dongle/adapter, Logi Bolt, Unifying receiver, LIGHTSPEED, HyperSpeed, HyperPolling Wireless Dongle, SLIPSTREAM, Quantum Wireless/2.0, SpeedNova, Bluetooth LE/Low Energy, BLE, and BT out of public chips.

Store only canonical count + type values. Add an unlisted <N> zone (rgb|led) only when direct evidence proves a real zone/programmable-LED count beyond the curated list; do not create chips from platform names or lighting-effect names. Keep Chroma RGB, Razer Chroma, LIGHTSYNC, PrismSync, iCUE, Aura Sync, RGB backlighting, RGB lighting zones, RGB LEDs, 16.8 million colors, individually programmable LEDs, underglow, light bar, status indicators, GameSense, and effect names out of chips.

Keep public chips to material or material-grade classes; accept newly evidenced stock feet materials with AI review instead of forcing them into PTFE or unk. Keep Teflon, mouse feet/skates/glide pad product names, Superglide/G-Skates/X'Glide/Armor Boot branding, PTFE grade aliases, UHMW-PE/UPE aliases, aluminosilicate glass, ceramic pad/skate wording, and distance/lifespan claims out of public chips unless they normalize to a canonical material value.

data_lists.normalize: canonical material class plus ptfe grade normalization

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Keep chips to factory hand-contact shell finish or built-in side grips. Normalize Standard Matte/matte finish/matte UV coating to matte, glossy/high-shine to glossy, full-shell rubberized/soft-touch/rubbery plastic to rubberized, Smooth-Touch Finish to smooth-touch, and integrated side grip inserts to rubber grips. Do not create new values from generic feel adjectives, aftermarket grip tape, cable/scroll-wheel/feet material, mousepad surface wording, or coating/color phrasing.

data_lists.normalize: lowercase canonical phrase; trim/collapse whitespace; preserve meaningful hyphens such as smooth-touch

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Treat this as a closed design/edition taxonomy, not a free-text aesthetic description. Keep source phrases such as special edition, signature edition, creator edition, artist edition, anniversary edition, team edition, player edition, co-branded, collab, partnered with, designed in collaboration with pros, pro-tested, exclusive design, exclusive drop, limited release, limited run, one-time-only release, serialized, collector item, limited-time colorway, colorway, aesthetic, shape, and ergonomic design out of public chips unless they map to one of the canonical values.

Keep the four curated chips for now; do not add brand cable-technology names unless curated as generic/material classes or intentionally public chips. Keep source phrases such as SpeedFlex/Speedflex cable, Ascended Cable/Cord, Flex Cord/Flex Cord 3.0, EZcord/EZcord Pro, PhantomFlex, HyperFlex, and soft/resistance-free/low-drag/flexible alone out of public chips.

Keep public chips to connector standards; do not create chips from marketing names, receiver names, cable branding, or generic USB wording. Normalize USB Type A/USB-A/Type-A/full-size USB to USB-A, USB Type C/USB-C/Type-C to USB-C, and PS2/PS/2/mini-DIN-6 to PS/2.

Normalize by the connector on the mouse body, not by cable marketing name, receiver/dongle connector, dock connector, or computer-side connector. Add Lightning as a known public chip for Apple Magic Mouse-era products.

Keep the public chip list small. Map source wording to the most specific existing material chip when exact: carbon fiber composite/CFRP -> carbon fiber; magnesium body/shell/chassis -> magnesium alloy; nylon outer shell with bio-based wording -> bio-based nylon; recycled/post-consumer/ocean plastic shell -> plastic unless a separate sustainability field or curated recycled-plastic chip is intentionally added. Prefer aluminum/magnesium alloy/carbon fiber/polycarbonate/ABS/PBT over generic metal/plastic when the source states the specific material. Keep shell/body/housing/chassis/enclosure/frame/construction words, sustainability percentages, paint/finish/coating/grip tape, feet/skates/PTFE, Gorilla Glass, cable/receiver/dongle, PCB/PWA, battery, and packaging out of public chips.

data_lists.normalize: lowercase; trim; collapse punctuation/hyphen variants; singular canonical material names

ui.label: Max Acceleration
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Max Acceleration' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Adjustable Scroll Wheel
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Adjustable Scroll Wheel' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Return true only when the exact mouse has a user-changeable physical weight or balance system: removable/movable/add-on weights, included tuning weights, optional official extra weight, or a branded tunable/center-of-gravity weight system. Do not count adjustable DPI/CPI, lift-off distance, scroll-wheel resistance/free-spin/tilt, DPI-shift button position, lighting/profiles, swappable side buttons/grips/shells, battery swaps, fixed weight, lightweight/reduced weight, or unofficial mods. For false, require exact-product evidence from an official/support/manual/retailer/spec source that gives a fixed weight and no weight system/included weights, or explicitly says no; otherwise return unk. If a family has mixed models, do not carry values between siblings or successors, for example G502 HERO vs G502 X.

ui.label: Adjustable Weight
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Adjustable Weight' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the manufacturer- or credible-source advertised maximum battery runtime for the exact mouse as a scalar hours value. Accept up to battery life/runtime/playtime/use-on-a-charge claims. If the source lists multiple modes or settings (2.4 GHz, Bluetooth, HyperSpeed, lighting on/off, polling rate, AA/AAA battery), emit the highest supported runtime that applies to the exact product because this field uses upper_bound; preserve the condition in evidence. Convert units to hours: days x 24, weeks x 168, months x 730, years x 8760; prefer explicit source hours when present. Do not use charge time, fast-charge minutes, battery capacity (mAh), standby/sleep time, or values from older/newer/sibling editions. For wired-only products or products with no defensible runtime evidence, return unk/no submitted value.

content_types: professional reviews with advertised or measured battery sections

ui.label: Battery Life (Hours)
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Battery Life (Hours)' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Return true only when the exact product/variant supports Bluetooth as a connection mode. Accept Bluetooth, Bluetooth Low Energy, Bluetooth LE, BLE, BT, Bluetooth Smart, or Bluetooth 4.x/5.x wording as true; version text is evidence only, not a separate value. Return false only when a credible product/spec/review source explicitly says no Bluetooth or enumerates all supported connection modes as wired/USB receiver/2.4 GHz/proprietary wireless only. Do not infer true from wireless, 2.4 GHz, USB receiver, dongle, LIGHTSPEED, HyperSpeed, or Wireless alone. If sources are generic, omit the exact variant, or conflict with sibling/edition evidence, return unk rather than guessing.

content_types: retailer spec tables/Q&A with manufacturer answers

ui.label: Bluetooth
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Bluetooth' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the cable sheath/flex class of the included cable(s), not connector, length, detachable status, wireless mode, or the separate paracord boolean alone. Prefer direct Cable/Cable Type/spec labels. Normalize exact terms: paracord/paracord-like/shoelace/lightweight paracord construction => paracord; rubber/rubber-coated/soft rubber cable => rubber; braided/double-sleeved/braided cable => braided; Cooler Master Ultraweave/Ultra-weave => ultraweave. Proprietary low-drag names such as SpeedFlex, Ascended, Flex Cord, EZcord, PhantomFlex, and HyperFlex need a material statement before mapping; do not create a new value from marketing alone. For products with multiple included/swappable cables, return every proven cable type. If sources only prove USB connectors, cable length, charging ability, or generic flexibility, return unk.

aliases: Ascended Cable -> paracord only when source says paracord material
SpeedFlex, Flex Cord, EZcord, PhantomFlex, HyperFlex -> marketing phrase unless material is also stated

ui.label: Cable Type
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Cable Type' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

Click Latency is a single numeric ms field, not a comma-delimited list.

ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Click Latency' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the factory exterior shell/hand-contact surface finish or coating, not body material, feet, cable, scroll-wheel rubber, mousepad surface, or aftermarket/in-box optional grip tape. Normalize Standard Matte/matte finish/matte UV coating to matte, glossy/high-shine to glossy, full-shell rubberized/soft-touch/rubbery plastic to rubberized, Smooth-Touch Finish to smooth-touch, and integrated side grip inserts to rubber grips. If a product family has official coating/color variants, return the union for the family page, but return only the exact coating for a known SKU/colorway/edition. In comparison tables, read the row/header context: ZOWIE Rubber commonly belongs to Cable and must not become coating. PIF images may support visible matte/glossy sheen or built-in rubber side panels, but do not infer UV/rubberized chemistry from images alone.

aliases: integrated side grip inserts -> rubber grips

content_types: product/PIF images for visible sheen or built-in side grips only

ui.label: Coating
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Coating' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Colors
ui.tooltip_md: Colorway tokens. Use list<string>. Normalize grey?gray; multi-colorways may use '+'.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the physical connector(s) that can plug into the computer for the exact mouse/package: cable computer end, wireless receiver/dongle plug, and any included adapter that changes the computer-facing plug. Do not use the mouse-side port (for example a USB-C charging port) unless the source also proves the computer-facing end is USB-C. Generic USB receiver, USB cable, or USB port is not enough for USB-A/USB-C unless the source text or a clear tied image proves the connector shape/type. Return multiple values only when the exact package includes multiple PC-facing plugs/adapters; do not union across separate connector variants/SKUs, and return unk when the requested variant is not pinned. Included adapters count; optional or third-party adapters do not.

match.format_hint: standards-style connector label, e.g. USB-A, USB-C, PS/2; avoid marketing names and generic USB-only labels

ui.label: Computer-side Connector
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Computer-side Connector' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Classify the exact mouse's computer connection mode, not its charging method, receiver extender, bundled cable, or switch technology. Return `wired` only when the exact product uses a cable for data/control and no wireless computer connection is supported. Return `wireless` when all usable computer connection modes are wireless protocols such as 2.4 GHz/RF dongle, Bluetooth/BLE, Logi Bolt/Unifying, LIGHTSPEED, HyperSpeed, or SLIPSTREAM; a USB port/cable listed only for charging or dongle extension is not wired mode. Return `hybrid` only when evidence explicitly supports both a wired USB data mode and at least one wireless computer connection mode; phrases like `tri-mode`, `wired + wireless`, `wireless/wired`, or a spec list containing both `Wired` and wireless protocols qualify. `Dual-mode wireless` (2.4 GHz + Bluetooth) is still `wireless`. Ignore `hybrid` when it describes switches, buttons, or sensor technology. Prefer detailed tech specs, manuals, or support setup guides over marketing titles and aggregator summaries. Reject sibling models/editions such as Pro, HyperSpeed, LIGHTSPEED, 35K, or wired-only variants unless the source matches the exact requested model/SKU.

ui.label: Connection
ui.tooltip_md: Primary connection mode (wired/wireless/hybrid/bluetooth). Prefer manufacturer docs.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the complete set of host connection modes for the exact product/edition/SKU, not only the default or most marketed mode. Map USB/USB-C/wired/data-cable mode to USB Wired only when the source says the mouse can operate over USB/wired mode; a charging-only cable is not enough. Map 2.4G/2.4 GHz/proprietary receiver or dongle modes, including LIGHTSPEED, HyperSpeed, SLIPSTREAM, Quantum, SpeedNova, Logi Bolt, Unifying, wireless receiver, and dongle language, to 2.4GHz RF Dongle; do not emit Wi-Fi unless the source explicitly describes Wi-Fi network connectivity. Map Bluetooth LE/BLE/BT to Bluetooth. Do not union across sibling wired/wireless/Bluetooth Edition/for Business variants; use the exact requested product evidence.

ui.label: Connectivity
ui.tooltip_md: Connectivity options (e.g., 2.4GHz, Bluetooth, USB). Use list if multiple.

ui.label: Debounce
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Debounce' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Classify the product's public visual/edition design, not ergonomic shape, color list, lighting, or material. Return `standard` for the ordinary/base retail design when no co-branded, limited-release, or multiple distinct artwork/edition designs apply. Return `collaboration` only for a co-branded/licensed/person/team/artist/creator edition or product name/source phrase such as `X x Y`, `Faker Edition`, `Sentinels Edition`, or `Aim Lab Edition` that gives the mouse a distinct design identity; do NOT treat generic phrases like `designed/developed in collaboration with pros`, `pro-tested`, or `feedback from esports athletes` as collaboration. Return `limited edition` when an official or credible source says limited edition/release/run, exclusive drop, serialized quantity, collector item, one-time-only release, or limited-time colorway for the exact variant. Return `multiple` only when the exact product record/series is intentionally sold with multiple distinct design/edition/artwork options; do not use it for ordinary color choices, RGB effects, or sibling models. If an exact variant is identified, classify that variant only; do not union sibling/base/special-edition values. A limited collaboration can return both `limited edition` and `collaboration`; dedupe and keep canonical values only. Use visual/PIF images as supporting evidence for co-branding, artwork, or standard appearance; missing images are not negative evidence.

ui.label: Design
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Design' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Discontinued
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Discontinued' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Max DPI
ui.tooltip_md: Max DPI (or max CPI) as integer. Do not list steps; store maximum unless field_rules later adds a steps field.

ui.label: Editions
ui.tooltip_md: Special/limited editions. Kebab-case slugs (e.g. cyberpunk-2077-edition).

ui.label: Encoder
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Encoder' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Encoder Link
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Encoder Link' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the installed/default glide feet/skates material for the exact mouse/SKU, not aftermarket replacements or optional accessory kits. Normalize Teflon to PTFE. Use PTFE (Virgin-Grade) only when the source says virgin, pure/100%, zero-additive, or equivalent; use PTFE (95%) only for 95%/>95% claims; use PTFE (Black-Dyed) only when feet/skates are explicitly black/dyed PTFE, not from mouse color. Treat glass, ceramic, UHMWPE/UHMW-PE/UPE, and metal/X'Glide armor boots as material classes when tied to feet/skates. If sources say PTFE or glass or list optional/in-box replacement skates without resolving the exact installed/default variant, return unk rather than unioning values. Strip thickness, shape, colorway, and lifespan/distance claims from the material value. Do not infer material from bottom images, shell material, or mousepad compatibility.

ui.label: Feet Material
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Feet Material' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Flawless Sensor
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Flawless Sensor' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Form Factor
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Form Factor' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Classify only the plan-view front/button-end outline. Return `outward` when the shell or main-button sides widen as they approach the front, `inward` when they narrow or tuck inward at the front, and `flat` when the front sidewalls are essentially straight or parallel. Normalize degree words such as slight/moderate/aggressive to the canonical direction; do not store intensity. Do not confuse front flare with side curvature, rear flare, thumb rest, ring-finger rest, or hump placement. Prefer explicit `Front flare` rows from shape databases; otherwise use official/top-view product images as `visual_inspection`. If the exact product image/wording is ambiguous, return `unk` rather than guessing.

ui.label: Front Flare
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Front Flare' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Hardware Acceleration
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Hardware Acceleration' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the mouse maximum physical vertical height/profile height in mm for the exact product/SKU/size. Prefer product/device dimensions on manufacturer pages, manuals, datasheets, and spec tables. Convert cm/in to mm and round to 1 decimal. Do not use package/box dimensions, receiver/dongle dimensions, button height, grip width, base size, or length/depth/width values. When a triplet is labeled H x W x D, take the first value; when labeled L x W x H or W x D x H, take the value mapped to H. If a source gives only an unlabeled triplet, or credible sources conflict on axis labels, corroborate with another labeled source or return unk. For families with multiple size variants, use the exact requested size/SKU; if the product identity does not resolve a single size, return unk instead of choosing a base/default.

query_terms: dimensions H x W x D
dimensions L x W x H

ui.label: Height
ui.tooltip_md: Overall height in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Treat honeycomb_frame=true when the exact mouse has a deliberate externally visible perforated, cut-out, holey, hex/honeycomb, mesh, skeletal, cage, or exoskeleton shell/frame on the main outer body; the hole pattern does not need to be hexagonal, and partial-body perforation counts when it is part of the top/side/main shell. Treat false when an explicit source says solid shell, removed all holes, or no honeycomb/cutouts, or when clear official/PIF product images show an unperforated top/side/body. Do not infer true from low weight alone, internal lattice, sensor/screw/vent openings, printed patterns, grip tape texture, or trivial underside-only openings. When family members differ, answer only the exact model/SKU/edition; if text is silent and images do not clearly show the shell, return unk rather than using absence as proof.

content_types: official product image gallery or PIF top/side/bottom images
retailer product details only as fallback

ui.label: Honeycomb Frame
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Honeycomb Frame' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Hot Swappable
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Hot Swappable' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Classify the longitudinal location of the mouse shell highest dorsal hump/apex, not overall height, grip suitability, hand size, front flare, or sensor position. Use the exact requested model/size/shell option; do not copy from sibling shapes. Map `front` only when the peak is clearly forward of center near the buttons/scroll-wheel area. Map `center` when the apex is near the midpoint with no meaningful rear bias. Map `back - minimal` for a slight rear bias or rear-centered peak; `back - moderate` for a clear rear-biased palm-support hump; and `back - aggressive` for a far-rear/back-of-palm peak. Treat source phrases such as rearward, rear-set, centered rear, middle, and mid-positioned as aliases that need normalization, not new enum chips. If a source only says high/low hump, low-profile, comfortable, or suited to a grip without proving placement, use images or another source; otherwise return unk.

content_types: official product pages and image galleries

ui.label: Hump
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Hump' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Max IPS
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Max IPS' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Lift-off Distance (LOD)
ui.tooltip_md: Lift-off distance (LOD) in mm. May be a range or set; normalize ranges to {min,max}.

ui.label: Lift Settings
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Lift Settings' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the mouse user-visible decorative/configurable lighting, not tiny DPI, battery, charging, or status indicators unless the manufacturer counts them in the lighting-zone total. Use (rgb) for Chroma/LIGHTSYNC/PrismSync/iCUE/Aura Sync/16.8M-color/customizable RGB wording; use (led) only for fixed or single-color lighting. Normalize manufacturer zones, individually programmable LEDs, and clearly counted RGB LED arrays to <N> zone (...); do not infer a zone count from generic RGB marketing alone. Keep to the exact model, edition, and SKU: family variants can differ, including Plus vs non-Plus, wired vs Pro, and translucent Phantom editions. For unresolved variant sets, use the exact requested/default/base product rather than unioning across editions. Images can support lit areas or no-lighting, but an unlit render is not negative evidence. Return none only with explicit no-RGB/no-lighting evidence or strong official visual/spec evidence; otherwise return unk.

ui.label: Lighting
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Lighting' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the mouse body front-to-back length in mm, excluding cable, dongles, packaging, charging dock, or hand-size references. Prefer explicit Length/L values from official specs. For dimension triples such as LxWxH, W x H x L, H/W/D, or Height/Width/Depth, map the value that corresponds to front-to-back body length, not vertical hump height; many mouse pages put the 100-130 mm body length under Height or Depth. Use sibling width/height values and a second source to confirm unlabeled or swapped-axis tables. Do not copy series/sibling sizes such as Mini/2c/HyperSpeed, EC1/EC2/EC3, or small/medium/large unless the source names the exact model; color-only variants usually share the same length. Convert cm/in to mm and round to 1 decimal.

ui.label: Length
ui.tooltip_md: Overall length in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the material(s) of the mouse primary external body/shell/chassis/enclosure for the exact product, edition, SKU, and colorway being requested. Special editions and sibling models can change material, so do not union across a product family. Return a list only for substantial built-in exterior materials on the exact product, such as a plastic shell plus built-in rubber side grip panels. Do not include paint, coating, rubberized finish, optional grip tape, mouse feet/skates, cable, receiver/dongle, PCB/PWA, battery, packaging, or sustainability percentages. Normalize housing/chassis/exoskeleton/enclosure/body/outer shell/source spelling to canonical material values; prefer the most specific proven material over generic plastic or metal. Do not infer plastic from a normal-looking mouse, light weight, or common market practice. If credible sources only prove coating/finish, weight, or appearance and do not state a material, return unk with the searched sources. Product/PIF images may help reject wrong variants, but they are not standalone proof unless on-page text/metadata explicitly identifies the material.

domain_hints: authorized retailer spec tables as fallback

ui.label: Material
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Material' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.tooltip_md: Count of middle buttons (wheel click + extra middle buttons).

ui.label: Motion Sync
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Motion Sync' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the connector or port on the mouse body used by a detachable cable for charging, data, or wired mode. Normalize by the mouse-end connector only: source phrases like USB Type A to USB Type C cable or USB-C charging/data cable mean USB-C only when the USB-C end plugs into the mouse; USB to Micro-USB or micro USB means Micro-B; Lightning to USB means Lightning. Use n/a only when the exact product has no detachable mouse-side port, such as a fixed/integrated wired cable, a replaceable-battery wireless mouse with no USB port, or a dock/pad-only design with no cable port. Do not use the computer-side plug, receiver/dongle connector, extension adapter, charging dock connector, Powerplay/Qi contacts, or generic cable_type as the value. If sources for the exact SKU/release disagree or mix old/new refreshes or wired/wireless siblings, return unk rather than unioning connector values.

aliases: USB Type A to USB Type C cable

content_types: authorized retailer spec table as fallback

ui.label: Mouse-side Connector
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Mouse-side Connector' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.tooltip_md: Define 'NVIDIA Reflex' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.

ui.label: Onboard Memory

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Set true only for the stock/included cable supplied with the exact mouse/variant when a source explicitly identifies it as paracord/paracord-like, says the cable is made from paracord material, or ties the named stock cable to paracord material/design for that product. Count wired mice and wireless mice whose included USB cable is used for charging or wired play, but ignore aftermarket replacement/mod kits unless the source says that cable is factory-installed or included with the product. Do not treat generic flexible, braided, cloth, low-drag, or cable-length wording as true by itself; proprietary names such as Speedflex, Ultraweave, Flex Cord, EZcord, PhantomCord, Ascended, HyperFlex, or Superflex need either a paracord/material statement or a credible review/spec that explicitly calls the cable paracord-like. False requires positive evidence for a standard/rubber/PVC/non-paracord cable or an explicit non-paracord/non-braided claim; do not infer false from silence. Use exact model/edition/SKU and skip sibling wired/wireless pages unless the source states the same cable applies.

content_types: official accessory page that defines the included stock cable
mouse spec database only as fallback/corroboration

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Boolean yes/no only; keep lighting-brand phrases such as Chroma, Razer Chroma, LIGHTSYNC, Prism, PrismSync, Brilliant Prism, Light Effects, Mouse Backlighting, and 16.8 million colors as aliases, not public chips. Return true only when the exact mouse is marketed or documented as having user-visible RGB lighting/backlighting/illumination, including source phrases such as RGB, Chroma RGB, LIGHTSYNC RGB, Prism/PrismSync, Brilliant Prism, per-LED RGB, full-spectrum lighting, customizable lighting, or 16.8M/16.8 million colors. Single-zone RGB still counts when the source calls it RGB or customizable color lighting. Return false when a credible source explicitly says no RGB/no lighting, or when official material clearly distinguishes this exact model from an RGB sibling and the RGB feature applies only to the sibling. Do not count DPI, battery, pairing, profile, or charging/status indicator LEDs as RGB lighting unless the product is also marketed as having RGB/backlighting. Product renders/photos can support true when lit zones are visible or captions/specs identify RGB; unlit photos alone are not negative evidence. Match exact edition/SKU/model suffix before copying an RGB claim, especially Plus/Pro/Ultimate/35K/Wired/Wireless/Gen 2 variants.

ui.label: RGB

ui.label: Sensor Brand

ui.guidance_md:
Extract the sensor component release/launch date, not the mouse product release date.
Normalize to ISO date. Use YYYY-MM-DD when exact day is known; use the first day of the known month/year when only month or year is available.
Hard constraint: sensor_date must be less than or equal to release_date. If evidence would make sensor_date later than release_date, reject that candidate and output unk with conflict reason instead.
ui.label: Sensor Date
ui.tooltip_md: Sensor release/launch date. Must be <= product release_date; reject later dates as conflicting evidence.

dataEntry.note: respoe time (fail safe js) MUST BE FILLED IN

ui.guidance_md:
Sensor Latency is a single numeric ms field, not a comma-delimited list.
Keep mode-specific measurements in sensor_latency_wired, sensor_latency_wireless, sensor_latency_bluetooth.
Only fill this scalar when a single authoritative value is reported or the source clearly names the primary mode value; otherwise output unk.
ui.label: Sensor Latency
ui.tooltip_md: Sensor Latency as one numeric ms value. Mode-specific values belong in the separate wired/wireless/bluetooth fields.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the structural shell shape, not hand compatibility, button handedness, or generic comfort marketing. Use `symmetrical` for mirrored/ambi-style shells even when the mouse is optimized for right-handed users or has side buttons only on the left. Use `ergonomic` for clearly non-mirrored/sculpted right- or left-handed shells; map source phrases such as `asymmetrical`, `asymmetric`, `ergo`, `right-handed ergonomic`, and `left-handed ergonomic` to this value. Use `hybrid` only when a credible shape source explicitly labels the product hybrid or describes an ambi/symmetrical top/front combined with ergonomic/asymmetric side/back shaping. Do not emit `ergonomic` merely because a source says the mouse is comfortable or has an ergonomic design in generic copy, especially when the same source or a stronger source calls the shell symmetrical/ambidextrous. For configurable-shell mice that support multiple structural shapes and have no single marketed/default shell, return unknown rather than forcing this scalar.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Count true only when the mouse has a dedicated molded thumb support area: a shelf, scoop, ledge, wing, platform, or extended side rest where the thumb can sit. Accept source phrases such as thumb rest, thumb scoop, thumb shelf, thumb ledge, thumb support, or extended thumb rest when tied to the exact mouse. Count false when product images or a credible shape/spec source show a normal side wall only, even if the mouse is ergonomic/asymmetrical. Do not treat thumb buttons, a thumb wheel, thumb-operated trackball, side grip texture, side curvature/groove, or generic thumb control as proof of a thumb rest by themselves. For negative values, prefer official/PIF images or a shape database with an explicit Thumb rest = No over mere absence of wording.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the mouse device/body weight in grams for the exact requested model/variant. Prefer manufacturer/support specs; use lab-measured default/actual weight when official specs are absent or clearly not a device weight. Treat weight without cable/excluding cable as the preferred convention; for wired mice, accept official mouse weight but do not subtract cable weight yourself. Exclude shipping/package weight, carbon-footprint kg CO2e, chargers/docks, receivers, grip tape/spare feet, and optional add-on weights. For adjustable-weight mice, store the stock/default body weight without optional add-on weights and let adjustable_weight capture the feature. If a source qualifies battery inclusion/exclusion, use the stated spec as-is and do not add/remove battery mass unless a total installed-battery value is directly given. When a family lists size/color/SKU-specific weights, return only the exact variant; do not average or emit a range. Convert oz/lb/kg to g, round per contract, and return unk if only sibling/family/package weight is proven.

ui.label: Weight
ui.tooltip_md: Mouse weight in grams. Convert oz?g when necessary; keep decimals if provided.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Extract the mouse body overall/maximum left-to-right width in millimeters for the exact model/SKU. Prefer official product/support/spec tables. If dimensions appear as LxWxH, take W; if a source labels Height/Width/Depth, use the value labeled Width even when the order is unusual. When a source gives multiple physical widths such as front/back, submit the widest body width unless an explicit overall width is listed. Do not use grip width, hand-contact width, package/shipping width, receiver/dongle width, cable length, or retailer axis labels that clearly swap length/depth/width. Convert cm/in to mm and round to 1 decimal. If sources conflict, prefer the latest brand-official page for the exact variant; use shape databases, review labs, and retailers only to corroborate or fill gaps. If the catalog row bundles multiple size variants without an exact size, return unk rather than averaging or listing widths.

ui.label: Width
ui.tooltip_md: Overall width in millimeters. Convert inches/cm to mm; require unit when parsing.

ai_assist.reasoning_note: Return true when the exact mouse can charge or power itself wirelessly through inductive/Qi, magnetic-field, proprietary charging-mat, dock-plus-puck/module, or named systems such as Logitech POWERPLAY/POWERPLAY 2/PowerCore, CORSAIR Qi/MM1000, Razer HyperFlux/HyperFlux V2, or Razer Mouse Dock Pro + Wireless Charging Puck. Accessory sold separately still counts true when official compatibility applies to the exact mouse; aftermarket adapters or generic magnetic USB cables do not make the base product true. Do not count ordinary USB charging, replaceable batteries, charging cables, or a contact/pogo-pin cradle as wireless charging unless the source explicitly says inductive/Qi/wireless charging. Phrases like wireless mouse charging dock may mean a dock for a wireless mouse, so verify whether charging uses contacts/pins versus a puck/coil. For false, prefer an explicit no/unsupported statement or close official power/charging documentation plus no compatible-wireless-charger listing; otherwise return unk. Match the exact submodel/edition and do not transfer values between siblings.

ui.label: Wireless Charging
ui.tooltip_md: Define 'Wireless Charging' according to contract. If not provable with evidence, output 'unk' with unknown_reason.
